honesty_phrases: Base model W is public but transformed to preserve hidden-state masks; the trusted side never exports plaintext A or B.
User input and token ids are trusted-side only and never leave the user device in plaintext.
GPU sees masked hidden states, masked LoRA adapters, masked LoRA gradients, and masked momentum buffers.
GPU does not see plaintext A/B, plaintext gradients, masks, or plaintext optimizer state.
AdamW under dense masks is unsupported; the module raises DenseMaskedAdamWUnsupported rather than approximating.
Masked-gradient LoRA provides algebraic equivalence for SGD/Momentum under orthogonal masks and proxy-evaluated leakage mitigation; it does not provide formal, cryptographic, or semantic security.

final_adapter_recovery_or_audit.exposed_form: A_pad = N_x A_tilde M^T, B_pad = M B_tilde N_y^T (trusted side)
final_adapter_recovery_or_audit.note: Recovery uses the orthogonal inverses; never executed on the GPU.
True rank slices used for downstream inference; never exposed to the GPU in plaintext.
Outputs publish summary scalars and short fingerprints to enable third-party audit without exposing raw tensors.

lora_initialization.note: Plaintext LoRA factors live only on the trusted side.
Orthogonal masks held only by trusted side.
Cancellation-padded factors: A_pad=[A_real, R, -R], B_pad=vstack(B_real, S, S); dummy contribution is zero at init.

lora_initialization.exposed_form: N_x^T A_pad M, M^T B_pad N_y
lora_initialization.note: Masked, rank-padded LoRA factors uploaded to the GPU. Visible padded rank, true rank hidden.

lora_initialization.exposed_form: transformed_to_preserve_hidden_state_masks (e.g., boundary linear absorbs N_x / N_y on trusted side)
lora_initialization.note: Base weights are publicly known but their use on the GPU is composed with the trusted-side mask boundary so that hidden-state masks survive the linear path.

masked_adamw_unsupported.note: Coordinate-wise second moments are not invariant under dense orthogonal mixing; AdamW under dense masks is unsupported.

masked_backward.note: MSE loss is computed against masked target. Orthogonal N_y preserves the L2 loss exactly. Plain target never leaves the trusted side.
Plaintext output gradient never crosses the boundary.
Equals 2 (Y - target) N_y / n; orthogonal masks commute through MSE.
Algebraic equivalence verified per step at float64 machine precision.
Algebraic equivalence verified per step at float64 machine precision.
Plaintext gradients never leave the trusted side.

masked_forward.note: Plaintext hidden state never crosses the boundary.
Only masked hidden state is uploaded.

masked_forward.exposed_form: N_x^T A M, M^T B N_y
masked_forward.note: GPU sees masked, rank-padded LoRA factors only.
Algebraically equals X A B N_y so the trusted side recovers Y by Y_tilde N_y^T.
Recovered only on the trusted side.

masked_momentum_sgd.exposed_form: V_tilde <- mu V_tilde + grad_tilde; param_tilde <- param_tilde - lr V_tilde
masked_momentum_sgd.note: Heavy-ball update is linear in the gradients; orthogonal masks commute, so masked momentum SGD is algebraically equivalent to plaintext momentum SGD after recovery.
Plaintext momentum buffers never live on the GPU; they can be recovered from the masked buffers on the trusted side only when needed.

masked_sgd.exposed_form: A_tilde - lr * grad_A_tilde, B_tilde - lr * grad_B_tilde
masked_sgd.note: Linear update; right-multiplication by orthogonal masks distributes, so masked SGD is algebraically equivalent to plaintext SGD after trusted-side recovery.
SGD has no persistent state besides the parameters.

trained_lora_inference.note: As in masked forward, tokens are trusted-side only.
GPU only sees the masked hidden state.

trained_lora_inference.exposed_form: N_x^T A_real_pad M, M^T B_real_pad N_y
trained_lora_inference.note: Trained masked LoRA factors uploaded for inference; true rank still hidden behind cancellation padding.
Base model is public but its composition with the trusted-side mask boundary preserves hidden-state masks at inference time.
Final output recovered on the trusted side by Y_infer = Y_infer_tilde @ N_y^T.

limitations: Lifecycle classification is descriptive; it is not a formal information-flow proof.

Base model W is public; we rely on a trusted-side boundary transformation to preserve hidden-state masks. A real deployment would have to verify the boundary transformation matches the trusted side's mask choice.
AdamW under dense masks is unsupported and is gated by DenseMaskedAdamWUnsupported in the ops module.
Raw tensors, masks, adapters, gradients, and optimiser states are NEVER exported.
paper_safe_wording: masked-gradient LoRA provides algebraic equivalence for SGD/Momentum under orthogonal masks and proxy-evaluated leakage mitigation; it does not provide formal, cryptographic, or semantic security.
